name: ◁ VEGAN DRIED FISH AND BITTER MELON SALAD

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) dried bean curd sticks, soaked
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) dried bean curd sticks, soaked

ingredients.item: 1¾ ounces (50 g) round leaf mint or mint, roughly chopped if leaves are large
ingredients.original: 1¾ ounces (50 g) round leaf mint or mint, roughly chopped if leaves are large

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) pickled scallion heads (optional; see Note)
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) pickled scallion heads (optional; see Note)

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE VEGAN DRIED FISH In a medium bowl, mash the chao cubes with a spoon until it’s a smooth paste. Add sugar and stock powder, and mix well. Add flour and stir briskly until you get a thick, smooth, homogenous mixture.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE VEGAN DRIED FISH In a medium bowl, mash the chao cubes with a spoon until it’s a smooth paste. Add sugar and stock powder, and mix well. Add flour and stir briskly until you get a thick, smooth, homogenous mixture.

ingredients.item: 2. Spread a thin layer of the mixture on each dried bean curd stick.
ingredients.original: 2. Spread a thin layer of the mixture on each dried bean curd stick.

ingredients.item: 3. Fill the bottom of a saucepan with about 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and place over medium heat. Check with a cooking thermometer and when oil reaches 320°F (160°C), add dried bean curd sticks and deep-fry until crisp and golden brown (1 to 2 minutes). Remove fried bean curd sticks, drain excess oil, and then cut into small pieces (about 2-inches/5-cm long).
ingredients.original: 3. Fill the bottom of a saucepan with about 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and place over medium heat. Check with a cooking thermometer and when oil reaches 320°F (160°C), add dried bean curd sticks and deep-fry until crisp and golden brown (1 to 2 minutes). Remove fried bean curd sticks, drain excess oil, and then cut into small pieces (about 2-inches/5-cm long).

ingredients.item: 4. MAKE THE SALAD Scrape all seeds and white spongy part off melons, slice them thinly crosswise, and then rinse a few times. (Optional: To reduce the bitterness, you can blanch the slices, and then drain.)
ingredients.original: 4. MAKE THE SALAD Scrape all seeds and white spongy part off melons, slice them thinly crosswise, and then rinse a few times. (Optional: To reduce the bitterness, you can blanch the slices, and then drain.)

ingredients.item:
5. Toss bitter melons, carrots, and salt in a large bowl, and let sit for 15 minutes.
6. Rinse, squeeze out excess water, and then place in a large bowl.
7. MAKE THE DRESSING In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, sugar, and lime juice, and stir well until sugar is dissolved.
8. Add the dressing to the bowl with the salad mixture and toss well.
9. Add mint, cilantro, dried fish, pickled scallion heads (if using), and roasted peanuts, and mix together.
10. Serve with sesame rice crackers (if using).
ingredients.original:
